title: Dynamic Programming States, Transitions, and Reconstruction
description: Optimize resource allocation where overlapping choices defeat local heuristics. Learners cumulatively produce a bottom-up optimizer with reconstructed decisions through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define minimal subproblem state that preserves all future-relevant information.
Derive transitions from exhaustive final choices and valid base cases.
Choose memoization or tabulation from reachability, order, depth, and memory needs.
